free_sample.description: 3-page sample pack used for lead generation via chatbot

pages.title: The Khanda
pages.theme: The central symbol of Sikhi representing divine knowledge, spiritual and temporal power
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A large cute rounded Khanda symbol at centre, decorated with simple chunky flower shapes and little hearts around it, surrounded by a thick circular border with big friendly lotus petals, warm cozy candles glowing on either side, everything soft and rounded, simple enough for a child to color
pages.educational_text: The Khanda is the emblem of the Sikh faith. The double-edged sword in the centre represents divine knowledge. The circle (Chakkar) symbolises God's eternal nature. The two curved swords (Kirpans) represent Miri and Piri — worldly and spiritual power.

pages.title: The Ik Onkar
pages.theme: The opening words of Guru Granth Sahib — there is One Universal Creator
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A big chunky Ik Onkar symbol in the centre drawn in a soft bubbly font style, surrounded by cute rounded paisley shapes and simple plump flowers, a cozy warm archway frame with rounded scalloped edges, tiny twinkling stars scattered around, everything soft and inviting
pages.educational_text: Ik Onkar means 'There is One God.' These are the opening words of the Mool Mantar, the foundational prayer in Guru Granth Sahib Ji. It teaches that the Creator is universal, without form, and present in all of creation.

pages.title: The Golden Temple — Harmandir Sahib
pages.theme: The holiest gurdwara in Sikhi, Amritsar, surrounded by the sacred pool
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A cute simplified Golden Temple with a big friendly rounded dome, chunky arched windows, a sweet little bridge walkway over gentle wavy water lines, big round marigold flowers and plump lotus blooms floating in the water, warm glowing lanterns on either side, everything bubbly and charming
pages.educational_text: Harmandir Sahib, known as the Golden Temple, sits in the holy city of Amritsar, Panjab. Built by Guru Arjan Dev Ji, it has four doors — one on each side — welcoming people of all backgrounds. The Langar hall serves free meals to over 100,000 visitors every day.

pages.title: Guru Nanak Dev Ji
pages.theme: The founder of Sikhi, spreading the message of equality and oneness
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A gentle cozy scene of Guru Nanak Dev Ji sitting peacefully cross-legged on a soft rounded cushion, wearing a simple robe and turban with a warm soft glow behind the head, one hand raised kindly, Bhai Mardana Ji sitting cosily beside playing a chunky rounded Rabab, soft rolling hills and a big friendly sunrise behind them, little birds and simple flowers around
pages.educational_text: Guru Nanak Dev Ji (1469–1539) is the founder of Sikhi. He travelled thousands of miles on foot across Asia, teaching that all people are equal regardless of caste, religion, or gender. His companion Bhai Mardana Ji accompanied him, playing sacred music.

gurmukhi_vocab.english: One God, whose name is Truth

pages.title: Guru Gobind Singh Ji & the Panj Piare
pages.theme: The tenth Guru and the creation of the Khalsa in 1699
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. Guru Gobind Singh Ji standing tall and proud with a big beautiful plumed turban, drawn in a soft rounded friendly style, five cute devoted figures (Panj Piare) gathered around looking up with love, a chunky Nishan Sahib flag waving gently in background, simple big flowers in the corners, everything warm and welcoming
pages.educational_text: In 1699 at Anandpur Sahib, Guru Gobind Singh Ji asked who would give their life for their faith. Five brave Sikhs stepped forward — they became the Panj Piare (Five Beloved Ones). Guru Ji created the Khalsa, a community of saint-soldiers committed to justice and equality.

pages.title: Guru Granth Sahib Ji
pages.theme: The eternal living Guru of the Sikhs — the holy scripture
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A cozy warm scene of Guru Granth Sahib Ji resting on a cute rounded Palki throne draped with soft chunky fabric, a big fluffy Chaur Sahib being gently waved above, sweet simple Rumala cloths with big bold simple patterns, warm glowing candles on either side, little flower petals scattered around, everything soft and reverent
pages.educational_text: Guru Granth Sahib Ji is the eternal Guru of the Sikhs. It contains 1,430 pages of sacred poetry and hymns composed by six Sikh Gurus and saints from Hindu, Muslim, and other traditions. Guru Gobind Singh Ji declared it the living Guru for all time.

pages.title: Seva — Selfless Service
pages.theme: The Sikh principle of serving others without expecting anything in return
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A warm cozy Langar kitchen scene with cute rounded characters happily cooking together, a big friendly bubbling pot on a chunky stove, one cute figure carrying a stack of round chapattis, another stirring with a big spoon, steam curling up in simple swirls, chunky bowls and plates stacked cosily, big smiles on everyone, warm and inviting
pages.educational_text: Seva means selfless service. In every Gurdwara, volunteers cook, clean, and serve free meals (Langar) to anyone who comes — regardless of their background. Seva teaches humility, compassion, and the joy of helping others without expecting reward.

pages.title: Chardi Kala — Eternal Optimism
pages.theme: The Sikh spirit of relentless positivity and resilience
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A cute smiling Sikh character wearing a cozy patka and wrapped in a big soft chunky blanket, sitting in a plush armchair by a rounded window, holding a steaming cup of Cha (Panjabi tea) in both hands, a beautiful big sunrise visible through the window, a happy cat curled up at their feet, everything warm and snuggly and round
pages.educational_text: Chardi Kala means 'ever-rising spirit.' It is the Sikh practice of maintaining optimism, courage, and joy even during life's hardest moments. Sikhs end their daily Ardas (prayer) by asking Waheguru for Chardi Kala for all of humanity.

pages.title: Miri Piri — Spiritual & Worldly Balance
pages.theme: The dual swords representing spiritual devotion and social responsibility
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. Two cute chunky crossed Kirpans in the centre drawn with rounded edges and soft curves, one wrapped in a simple prayer bead mala, the other with a little rounded shield, surrounded by a simple balanced design with a lotus flower on one side and helping hands on the other, cozy candles glowing at the bottom, chunky stars and hearts scattered around
pages.educational_text: Miri Piri means worldly authority and spiritual authority. Guru Hargobind Ji wore two swords to show that Sikhs must be both devoted in prayer and active in protecting the weak. A Sikh is a saint-soldier: compassionate in spirit and courageous in action.

pages.title: Sarbat Da Bhala — Welfare of All
pages.theme: The Sikh prayer for the wellbeing of all humanity
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. Adorable cute rounded children from different backgrounds sitting cosily together on a big soft blanket having a picnic, sharing food from chunky bowls, a cute Sikh child in a round dastar in the middle passing out treats, big plump doves and simple flowers around them, a warm sun smiling above, everything bubbly and friendly
pages.educational_text: Every Sikh prayer ends with 'Nanak Naam Chardi Kala, Tere Bhane Sarbat Da Bhala' — 'With your blessing, may there be prosperity for all.' Sikhs believe in working for the good of every person on Earth, not just their own community.

pages.title: Kesh — Uncut Hair
pages.theme: Respecting the natural form God has given, crowned with the Dastar (turban)
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A cute happy Sikh character sitting at a cozy vanity mirror carefully tying a big beautiful rounded Dastar turban, long flowing Kesh hair drawn in simple chunky waves, three cute little vignette circles showing different turban styles (Patiala Shahi, Dumalla, Patka), a warm cup of tea on the vanity, everything soft and proud and round
pages.educational_text: Kesh (uncut hair) is the first of the Five Ks. Sikhs keep their hair unshorn as a gift from Waheguru, respecting the body's natural form. The Dastar (turban) is worn with pride to cover and protect the Kesh — it represents sovereignty, honour, and self-respect.

pages.title: The Five Kakars
pages.theme: The five articles of faith worn by initiated Sikhs
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. The Five Ks arranged beautifully on a chunky rustic wooden table: a cute rounded Kangha comb, a thick friendly Kara bracelet, a small sweet Kirpan in its sheath, neatly folded Kachera, and flowing Kesh hair with a comb in it, a tiny warm glowing candle in the centre of the table, simple flowers in a little vase, everything cozy and clean and bold
pages.educational_text: The Five Ks (Panj Kakars) are articles of faith: Kesh (uncut hair), Kangha (wooden comb — discipline), Kara (steel bracelet — bond with God), Kachera (cotton undergarment — self-control), and Kirpan (ceremonial sword — duty to protect the weak).

pages.title: Vaisakhi — The Harvest Festival
pages.theme: Celebrating Vaisakhi, the Panjabi new year and birth of the Khalsa
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A joyful cute Vaisakhi celebration scene with adorable rounded characters doing Bhangra dance, a happy chunky dhol drummer in the centre with big round cheeks, cute bunting flags and big round marigold flowers everywhere, chunky wheat sheaves in the foreground, everyone wearing simple rounded traditional Panjabi clothes, big happy smiles
pages.educational_text: Vaisakhi is celebrated on April 13th or 14th each year. It marks the Panjabi harvest festival and, for Sikhs, the birth of the Khalsa in 1699. Communities celebrate with Nagar Kirtan (street processions), Bhangra dancing, and sharing festive foods.

pages.title: The Gurdwara
pages.theme: The Sikh place of worship — open to all
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A warm cozy indoor scene of a cute Sikh family sitting together cross-legged on a beautifully patterned simple chunky rug, listening peacefully to Kirtan, a cute rounded harmonium in front, little children sitting cosily with their parents, warm glowing lanterns on either side, soft cushions scattered around, everything warm and inviting and homely
pages.educational_text: A Gurdwara ('Gateway to the Guru') is the Sikh place of worship. It has four doors to show it is open to people of every direction and background. Inside, the Guru Granth Sahib Ji is read, Kirtan (hymns) are sung, and everyone shares Langar together.

pages.title: Phulkari — Flower Embroidery
pages.theme: The traditional Panjabi art of floral embroidery on textiles
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A full-page chunky bold Phulkari embroidery pattern with big simple diamond shapes filled with cute rounded stars and chubby stylized flowers, sweet little peacock motifs with big round tail feathers, chunky wheat stalks, arranged in a simple symmetrical grid, everything big and bold and easy to color with thick heavy outlines
pages.educational_text: Phulkari means 'flower work.' It is the traditional embroidery art of Panjab, created by women using bright silk threads on handspun cotton cloth. Each region of Panjab has its own patterns. A Bagh ('garden') is a Phulkari so densely stitched that no base fabric shows through.

pages.title: Panjabi Alphabet — Gurmukhi
pages.theme: The sacred script created by Guru Angad Dev Ji to write the Panjabi language
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A cozy study scene with a cute Sikh child sitting at a chunky wooden desk practising writing Gurmukhi letters, a few large friendly bubbly Gurmukhi letters floating above like thought bubbles, an open chunky book on the desk, a warm cup of Cha beside them, a cozy lamp glowing, a happy little stack of books, everything round and warm and studious
pages.educational_text: Gurmukhi means 'from the mouth of the Guru.' This script was standardised by Guru Angad Dev Ji so that everyone could read and write, regardless of caste. The Guru Granth Sahib Ji is written in Gurmukhi. The alphabet has 35 letters and is written left to right.

gurmukhi_vocab.english: From the Guru's mouth / Panjabi script

pages.title: Mai Bhago — The Warrior Saint
pages.theme: The legendary Sikh woman warrior who led soldiers in battle
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A strong cute Mai Bhago character drawn in a friendly rounded style, standing proudly with a chunky rounded spear, wearing a beautiful big turban and flowing dupatta, a cute chunky horse standing loyally beside her, big simple flowers and rounded shields decorating the border, everything bold and empowering but soft and friendly, big confident smile
pages.educational_text: Mai Bhago was a courageous Sikh warrior who led 40 soldiers back into battle at Muktsar in 1705. She fought alongside Guru Gobind Singh Ji and survived the battle. She is remembered as a symbol of bravery and a reminder that Sikh women have always been warriors and leaders.

pages.title: The Sahibzade — Sons of Guru Gobind Singh Ji
pages.theme: Honouring the four young sons who gave their lives for their faith
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. Four brave cute young boys standing together in a warm protective group, wearing sweet little rounded turbans and simple traditional attire, the two taller boys standing behind the two smaller ones with arms around them lovingly, a glowing chunky Khanda symbol above them like a warm light, simple lotus flowers and gentle flame shapes around them, everything respectful and tender and round
pages.educational_text: The Sahibzade are the four sons of Guru Gobind Singh Ji. Sahibzada Ajit Singh (18) and Jujhar Singh (14) fought bravely in battle. Sahibzada Zorawar Singh (9) and Fateh Singh (6) chose death over giving up their faith. Their courage inspires Sikhs to stand firm in their beliefs.

gurmukhi_vocab.english: Revered sons / princes of the Guru

pages.title: Sikh Mandala — Sacred Geometry
pages.theme: A meditative geometric pattern incorporating Sikh symbols
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. A big friendly chunky mandala design with a cute rounded Khanda at the centre, radiating outward with rings of simple bold lotus petals, chunky paisley shapes, big round stars, and plump flower shapes, everything symmetrical and satisfying with thick heavy outlines, simple enough to color easily but beautiful and meditative, warm and inviting
pages.educational_text: Mandalas represent the universe in sacred art traditions. This mandala weaves together symbols of Sikhi into a pattern for meditation and reflection. As you colour each ring, reflect on the values they represent: truth, compassion, contentment, humility, and love.

pages.title: Waheguru — Colour Your Blessing
pages.theme: A final celebratory page with the word Waheguru as the centrepiece
pages.ai_prompt: Bold and easy coloring page, thick clean black outlines, minimalist kawaii line art style, cozy hygge atmosphere, rounded cute shapes, solid white background, flat vector design, no shading, no gradients, heavy line weight. The word Waheguru written in big chunky bubbly Gurmukhi letters as the centrepiece, each letter filled with simple cute patterns like hearts, stars, and flowers, surrounded by a warm glow of simple sunburst lines, four cozy corner vignettes: a cute child meditating on a cushion, hands sharing food, a plump blooming lotus, and a chunky open book, chunky stars and hearts scattered everywhere, warm and celebratory
pages.educational_text: Waheguru means 'Wonderful Lord' — it is the Sikh name for God. Sikhs repeat Waheguru in meditation (Simran) to feel connected to the divine presence in everything. You have now journeyed through the beauty of Sikh heritage. Waheguru Ji Ka Khalsa, Waheguru Ji Ki Fateh!
